sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Las Palmas are in poor form, losing 4 of their last 5 matches with only 1 goal scored in their last 4 games. Leganés, while also struggling, managed a win and a draw in their last 5, showing slightly better attacking output with 6 goals scored in that span.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In the last two head-to-head matches, Leganés won 2-1 at home in August 2024, while the other game ended in a 0-0 draw. Matches between these teams tend to be tight, often with low-scoring outcomes.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Both teams are near the bottom of the league table, with Las Palmas at 19th (40 goals scored, 58 conceded) and Leganés at 18th (35 goals scored, 56 conceded). Las Palmas struggle defensively, ranking 19th in goals conceded, while Leganés show marginally better balance.

sections.subtitle: Key Players and Injuries
sections.text: Las Palmas miss key players like Fábio Silva (10 goals), impacting their attack, while Leganés have Dani Raba (7 goals, 6 assists) in form. This could tilt the attacking edge slightly towards Leganés.

sections.subtitle: Match Dynamics
sections.text: Given the referee's high yellow card average (5.6 per game) and historical head-to-head trends (over 4.5 cards in 7/8 matches), expect a physical game. Both teams average low corner counts, aligning with recent trends of under 10.5 corners.
